title: machaeranthera viscida
intro: Machaeranthera viscida, also known as Sticky Aster, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Machaeranthera viscida include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for topical application, although its use should be limited to external purposes and under proper guidance.
